# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Redis configuration for our cloud-deployed application. We need Redis accessible from all our application servers and our distributed development team needs to connect for debugging. Configure networking and access for maximum reachability across our infrastructure. Use default port 6379. This is for our development and staging environments.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis configuration for development/staging environments
#
# NETWORK & ACCESS:
#   - Binds to all available network interfaces (0.0.0.0) for maximum reachability.
#   - `protected-mode no` allows connections from external IPs without a password.
#     WARNING: This is INSECURE for production. Use for dev/staging with appropriate firewall rules.
#   - Default port 6379.
#
# IMPORTANT: This configuration is highly permissive for development/staging ease.
#            Ensure your cloud provider's firewall/security groups are configured
#            to restrict access to only necessary IPs (e.g., your application
#            servers' IPs, your development team's VPN IPs/public IPs).
#            NEVER use this exact configuration in production without strong security measures.

# General
daemonize yes
pidfile /var/run/redis_6379.pid
port 6379

# Networking
bind 0.0.0.0
protected-mode no

# Logging
loglevel notice
logfile /var/log/redis/redis_6379.log

# Snapshotting (Persistence)
# Save the DB every 900 seconds (15 minutes) if at least 1 key changed
save 900 1
# Save the DB every 300 seconds (5 minutes) if at least 10 keys changed
save 300 10
# Save the DB every 60 seconds if at least 10000 keys changed
save 60 10000

stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes
rdbcompression yes
rdbchecksum yes
dbfilename dump.rdb
dir /var/lib/redis/6379

# Replication
# Disable diskless replication for simplicity in dev/staging
repl-diskless-sync no

# Security
# WARNING: No password set for maximum ease of access in dev/staging.
#          HIGHLY RECOMMENDED to set a password even in dev/staging
#          if accessible over the public internet.
# requirepass your_strong_dev_password_here

# Client limits
maxclients 10000

# Memory Management
# Set a reasonable max memory limit for your environment.
# Example: 2GB for a dev/staging instance. Adjust as needed.
maxmemory 2gb
maxmemory-policy noeviction

# Append Only File (AOF) persistence
appendonly no
# appendfsync always
# appendfsync everysec
# appendfsync no

# LUA scripting
lua-time-limit 5000

# Slow log
slowlog-log-slower-than 10000
slowlog-max-len 128

# Latency monitoring (Redis 4.0+)
latency-monitor-threshold 0

# Cluster configuration (not applicable for standalone instance)
cluster-enabled no

# Active defragmentation (Redis 4.0+)
activedefrag yes

# TLS/SSL (disabled by default for simplicity in dev/staging)
# tls-port 6379
# tls-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.crt
# tls-key-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.key
# tls-ca-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
# tls-auth-clients yes
# tls-ciphers-server DEFAULT:!DHE
# tls-ciphers-client DEFAULT:!DHE

# Event notification (Keyspace notifications)
# Set to 'E' for events related to Keyspace events (keys deleted/expired)
# and 'g' for generic commands. Adjust as needed.
notify-keyspace-events Eg
